title: chore(tooling): add Size Limit
body:
This is a proposal to replace [Bundlesize](https://github.com/siddharthkp/bundlesize) by [Size Limit](https://github.com/ai/size-limit) in our tooling to track bundle size increases/decreases.

## Output

[See CI output →](https://circleci.com/gh/algolia/instantsearch.js/2691)

### Success

```
$ size-limit

  UMD
  Size limit:   80 KB
  Package size: 77.89 KB with all dependencies, minified and gzipped
  Loading time: 1.6 s    on slow 3G
  Running time: 932 ms   on Snapdragon 410
  Total time:   2.5 s

  CJS
  Size limit:   100 KB
  Package size: 91.56 KB with all dependencies, minified and gzipped
  Loading time: 1.9 s    on slow 3G
  Running time: 1.7 s    on Snapdragon 410
  Total time:   3.5 s

  ESM
  Size limit:   100 KB
  Package size: 90.11 KB with all dependencies, minified and gzipped
  Loading time: 1.9 s    on slow 3G
  Running time: 1.3 s    on Snapdragon 410
  Total time:   3.1 s
```

### Failed

```
$ size-limit

  UMD
  Package size limit has exceeded by 7.89 KB
  Size limit:   70 KB
  Package size: 77.89 KB with all dependencies, minified and gzipped
  Loading time: 1.6 s    on slow 3G
  Running time: 772 ms   on Snapdragon 410
  Total time:   2.4 s

  CJS
  Size limit:   100 KB
  Package size: 91.56 KB with all dependencies, minified and gzipped
  Loading time: 1.9 s    on slow 3G
  Running time: 1.5 s    on Snapdragon 410
  Total time:   3.3 s

  ESM
  Size limit:   100 KB
  Package size: 90.11 KB with all dependencies, minified and gzipped
  Loading time: 1.9 s    on slow 3G
  Running time: 1.2 s    on Snapdragon 410
  Total time:   3 s

  Try to reduce size or increase limit in .size-limit.js
```

## Comparison with Bundlesize

### Pros

- Works for UMD, CJS and ESM bundles
- Shows loading time
- Shows running time

### Cons

- No complete GitHub integrations with reports in checks
- Not able to compare with base branch

The major downside that I see is that it doesn't compare the current branch bundle size with the base branch. However, Bundlesize worked only based on `master`, which was meaningless for us.

Therefore I think this tool brings more value than Bundlesize for now. Let me know what you think!
